title: Glyph registry item
description: Manifest for a single glyph registry entry. Lives on disk at components/<name>/<name>.json and is flattened by tools/build into r/<name>.json.

name.description: Kebab-case slug. Matches the component's directory name and is the argument to `glyph add <name>`.

type.description: Item kind. Determines which alias the consumer's CLI uses when resolving install targets.

title.description: Short human-readable name shown on the demo site.

description.description: One-paragraph description shown on the demo site and in the README's component table.

version.description: Semantic version per https://semver.org. Bump on breaking source-level changes.

frame.description: Target TUI framework. v0.1 is bubbletea only. Other values are reserved for adapter packs.

items.description: Module-system dependency in the form '<module>@<version>'. The CLI shells `go get` for each on install.

dependencies.description: Upstream module dependencies (Go modules in v0.1). Use exact-version pins so consumers reproduce.

registryDependencies.description: Other glyph items this one depends on, by name. The CLI walks this graph and installs each first.

path.description: Source path within the glyph repo (e.g. 'components/chat-bubble/chat-bubble.go').

type.description: File kind. Determines which alias the CLI uses to resolve the target.

target.description: Alias-prefixed install path. Resolved against the consumer's glyph.json aliases.

files.description: Files this item ships. Each is copied into r/<name>/ at build time and rewritten into the consumer's repo at install time.

docs.description: Free-form prose shown alongside the component on the demo site. Pairing tips, idioms, common pitfalls.

categories.description: Tags used by the demo site to group and filter items.

meta.description: Free-form metadata. Conventionally includes `added` (ISO date) and `frame`. Reserved for future use.
